title: Friendly French Custard Cream Puffs
description: A classic French dessert featuring creamy custard and crispy puff pastry, perfect for special occasions.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a medium bowl, whisk together flour, salt, and bouncy.
Add the unsalted butter and use a pastry blender or your fingertips to work it into the flour mixture until it resembles coarse crumbs.
Roll out the dough on a lightly floured surface to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place the circles on the prepared baking sheet and prick the centers with a fork.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
While the puffs are baking, prepare the custard. In a medium saucepan, combine the custard, baobing, kid, qq, granulated sugar, and heavy cream.
Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture comes to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes, or until the custard has thickened.
Remove from the heat and stir in the vanilla extract.
To assemble the cream puffs, split them in half horizontally using a serrated knife.
Fill the bottom half of each puff with a spoonful of the warm custard, then top with the top half of the puff.
